column1: # TOMBSTONES.tsv -- the ENFORCED resurrection-blocklist (S-2026-06-24L).
# WHY: engines kept getting un-tombstoned. GoldOrbRetrace was resurrected THREE times
# -- each time someone added a fresh "EDGE"/PF row and the registry's DEAD/UNAUDITED
# check went green, so a live-falsified engine traded again. cull_audit.py logs culls
# but NEVER blocks (exit 0 always). engine_registry blocks UNAUDITED/DEAD-but-enabled
# but a new AUDITED_CONFIGS row silences it. Nothing made a tombstone STICK.
# WHAT: this file is the hard blocklist. tools/tombstone_guard.py (wired into the mac
# canary) scans engine_init.hpp for `g_X.enabled = true` and FAILS the build if any
# tombstoned global here is enabled -- and it OVERRIDES AUDITED_CONFIGS, so adding a
# fresh EDGE row CANNOT resurrect a FORBIDDEN engine. The ONLY way to re-enable is to
# edit THIS file (a deliberate, reviewed, git-logged act) -- which is the friction.
# policy:
#   FORBIDDEN            -- dead at source / live-falsified. Do NOT re-enable. Ever.
#                           To revisit: remove the row here (logged) AND attach a FRESH
#                           faithful-TICK PF (not bar-replay, not a slice) in the commit.
#   FAITHFUL-BT-REQUIRED -- disabled bull-beta / marginal / un-gated. Re-enable ONLY with
#                           a faithful-tick BT row in AUDITED_CONFIGS + downgrade this row.
# Re-enabling ANY row below = canary FAIL. That is intentional. Resurrection must be loud.
# columns: global <TAB> engine <TAB> tombstone_date <TAB> basis <TAB> policy <TAB> reason
g_gold_orb_retrace
column6: live ledger 7/7 losses net -$808 (all sub-spread TRAIL_STOP) = intraday-spot-cfd cost wall. UN-TOMBSTONED 3x already (15/16-06, 23-06). Faithful 1.86 was bull-only + bear-untestable. Commit a96d8e02. DO NOT un-tombstone a 4th time without a faithful-TICK edge.
